item.title: Centrica CEO Eyes US for More LNG Deals and Trading Expansion
item.description: The UK’s Centrica Plc plans to buy more US liquefied natural gas, as it sees the fuel remaining a key part of the energy system for decades even as global geopolitical tensions highlight the need to diversify supplies.

item.title: A Blockbuster Summer for Debt Issuance
item.description: Corporate bond bonanza.

item.title: Alphabet Shares Drop After Second AI Star Departs for Rival
item.description: Alphabet Inc. shares tumbled on Monday following the departure of another high-profile artificial intelligence leader to a rival.

item.title: Junk-Bond Sales Begin to Help Fund Castrol, Amex GBT Buyouts
item.description: A pair of junk-bond deals to help fund leveraged buyouts hit the market on Monday, after concurrent loan offerings were launched last week to partially finance those acquisitions.

item.title: Chinese Carmakers Weighing Canada Manufacturing Deals, Joly Says
item.description: At least four Chinese carmakers are looking at how to use Canada’s low-tariff import quota for their electric vehicles and are interested in domestic manufacturing partnerships, Industry Minster Melanie Joly said.

item.title: Paragon Debt Targeted by Deutsche Bank Analysts Over Deal Spree
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG analysts are recommending investors short Paragon’s debut bond, saying the UK business services group is overly dependent on acquisitions for growth.

item.title: Kazakh $1 Billion Fund Backs Startups as Region Lures Investment
item.description: Kazakhstan’s Alem Capital Management anchored a regional venture capital fund backing technology startups across Central Asia and the Caucasus.

item.title: SpaceX Inks Multibillion-Dollar Computing Deal With Reflection AI
item.description: SpaceX has inked a multibillion-dollar agreement to provide computing resources to artificial intelligence startup Reflection AI, the latest in a string deals from Elon Musk’s firm as it vies to become an AI infrastructure provider.

item.title: Heat Wave Lowers Rhine Levels, Straining Fuel Supply Chains
item.description: Falling Rhine water levels are choking fuel shipments to parts of western Europe as a heat wave grips the region, forcing barges to sail with less than half their normal loads.

item.title: Brazil Moves to Calm Markets After ‘Confusing’ Rate Decision
item.description: Brazil is stepping up efforts to ease investor concerns after a confusing central bank decision, with the Treasury canceling a scheduled domestic bond auction and the monetary authority moving to inject liquidity into the spot currency market as local rates sell off.

item.title: Two Plead Guilty to Hacking Transport for London While Teenagers
item.description: Two British men pleaded guilty to carrying out a cyber attack on London’s transport network while still teenagers.

item.title: Vance Hails ‘Good Day’ of Iran Talks as US Waives Oil Sanctions
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance said talks with Iran over the weekend were “very, very good,” as the sides attempt to reach a peace deal within two months and formally end a war that’s reverberated through the global economy.

item.title: Silver Miner Sinda Seeks $345.2 Million in US IPO, Placement
item.description: Sinda Ltd., a silver mining company, is looking to raise as much as $345.2 million in a US initial public offering and private placement, as firms seek to capitalize on rising metals prices.

item.title: Argentina Approves Dollar Borrowing Amid Multilateral Loan Talks
item.description: The Argentine government authorized up to $5 billion in new dollar-denominated borrowing as the country looks to secure funding backed by multilateral institutions ahead of upcoming debt payments.

item.title: Qatar Says Explosion at Ras Laffan Site Won’t Affect LNG Exports
item.description: Qatar said it doesn’t expect liquefied natural gas exports to be impacted by an explosion at the Ras Laffan industrial complex which left 13 dead and 66 injured.

item.title: Senegal Trade Minister Says Government Open to Debt Restructure
item.description: Senegal is open to discussing a debt restructuring as mounting payment pressures threaten to strain key sectors of the economy, according to a senior member of President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s cabinet.

item.title: Groq Raises $650 Million to Aid in Pivot After Nvidia Deal
item.description: Groq Inc. raised $650 million in a new funding round aimed at expanding its data center capacity and helping the one-time chip startup become a provider of artificial intelligence computing.

item.title: Lagarde Sees No Need for More Forceful ECB Response to War
item.description: The European Central Bank doesn’t need to react more forcefully to the fallout from the Middle East conflict because inflation is set to return to target over the medium term, President Christine Lagarde said.

item.title: Nvidia Seeks to Make Humanoid AI Robots Safer Around Humans
item.description: Nvidia Corp. is working to make humanoid robots safer around people, arguing that they’ll need to handle split-second decisions before they can be trusted to work closely with humans.

item.title: Meta Taps New WhatsApp Boss as Part of $900 Million Investment
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is investing $900 million into Indian fintech startup Cred, with plans to appoint its founder, Kunal Shah, the new leader of WhatsApp.

item.title: Mexico Is Tapping Global Debt Markets to Fund Bond Buyback
item.description: Mexico is tapping global bond markets to fund a buyback of its existing debt as pressure mounts from credit-rating agencies to reign in the government’s deficit.

item.title: Bond Traders Plot Impact of Next UK Chancellor With Starmer Out
item.description: UK bond investors say Chancellor Rachel Reeves is on borrowed time after Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation and are plotting the market impact of her possible replacements.

item.title: Gasoline Spike Pushes Canada Inflation to Highest Since 2023
item.description: Spiking gasoline prices pushed Canadian inflation to the highest in over two years, while the breadth of price pressures narrowed and core measures were little changed.

item.title: Morocco’s World Cup Czar Urged to Run for Premier in Fall Vote
item.description: The man who helped transform Morocco’s football team into a serious World Cup contender is being urged to focus on a new goal: the role of prime minister.

item.title: Robinhood Seeks to Raise $2 Billion in Convertible Bond Sale
item.description: Robinhood Markets Inc. is seeking to raise $2 billion in a convertible bond offering as it joins a roaring market for pooling cash.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Buys $34.9 Million of Bitcoin Using Common Stock
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $39.4 million of Bitcoin, leaning again on its common stock to fund purchases of the cryptocurrency for a third consecutive week despite earlier pledges to pivot to perpetual preferred shares.

item.title: SpaceX Bankers Kick Off Marketing for Debut High-Grade Bond Sale
item.description: SpaceX is selling investment-grade bonds for the first time in what’s expected to be the start of a massive borrowing spree to fund the company’s AI ambitions following its record $75 billion IPO.

item.title: Oil Is Starting to Flow Through Hormuz Despite Iran’s Warnings
item.description: Estimates vary, but more crude is clearly moving out of the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Copper Rallies as Peace-Talk Progress Eases Inflation Fears
item.description: Copper prices rose after Iran said there had been “major progress” in peace talks with the US, boosting expectations that traffic through the Strait of Hormuz may recover and ease inflation pressures.

item.title: Bomar Leaves Deutsche Bank to Lead New AI-Driven Advisory Falkon
item.description: Investment banker Derek Bomar has left Deutsche Bank AG to set up a financial and professional services firm focused on investment management called Falkon Partners.
